methods.method_name: Measurement of Radioactivity and Determination of Health Risks
methods.description: This method involves measuring radionuclides in sediments using a gamma spectrometer and calculating various indices to assess radiation hazards.

steps.description: Radionuclides in the sediments were measured with a gamma spectrometer equipped with HPGe detector (Ortec GWL-120230 MJ-GWL).
The energy and efficiency of the detector were calibrated with IAEA Soil 6 reference material.
For statistical confidence, the counting time of marine sediment was optimized to 60,000-80,000 s.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize counting time for statistical confidence

steps.description: The radioactivity was calculated by: A = Rn / (ε * Pγ * W).

To assess the radiation hazards, the absorbed dose rate in air (D), the radium equivalent activity (Raeq), the external hazard index (Hex), and the annual external effective dose rate (AEDout) were calculated from activities of U-238, Th-232 and K-40 using equations 2-5.

methods.method_name: Analysis of Heavy Metals by ICP-AES
methods.description: This method describes the digestion of marine sediment and benthos samples for the analysis of heavy metals using ICP-AES.

steps.description: 1 g of dried sediments was digested in 10 mL of 8 M nitric acid over a hot plate at 92-95°C for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sediment in nitric acid on hot plate

steps.description: After cooling, 5 mL of concentrated nitric acid was added with continuous heating for 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: add concentrated nitric acid and heat

steps.description: Deionized water (2 mL) and 30% hydrogen peroxide (3 mL) were added; the digested sediment was then heated until bubbles disappeared.
steps.searchable_summary: add water and hydrogen peroxide, heat until bubbles disappear

steps.description: Hydrochloric acid (6 M, 10 mL) and 2 mL of deionized water were then added; the mixture was heated for 10 min, then left to cool and filtered through Whatman paper no. 42.
steps.searchable_summary: add hydrochloric acid and water, heat, cool, and filter

steps.description: The solution was adjusted to 100 mL with deionized water.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust solution volume with deionized water

steps.description: Concentrations of heavy metals As, Cd, Cr, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb and Zn were determined by a precalibrated ICP-AES (JY 2000, Jobin Yvon) with detection limits of 2.82, 0.32, 4.49, 0.002, 0.05, 0.12, 0.007 and 0.05 ppm, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: determine heavy metal concentrations using ICP-AES

steps.description: Benthos samples were digested and analyzed in the same manner as the sediments, following classification of the benthic communities.
steps.searchable_summary: digest and analyze benthos samples similarly
